exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="left")
print(len(m))
exercises.note: Starter gives cli/tx; left merge keeps 2 rows.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C001"]})
print(bool(cli["cliente_id"].is_unique))
exercises.note: Hints is_unique; starter has dups → False.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C001", "C001"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="inner")
print(len(m))
exercises.note: Instruction 1 cliente x 3 tx → len 3 inner.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002", "C003"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="left", indicator=True)
print(m.loc[m["_merge"] == "left_only", "cliente_id"].tolist())

import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C001"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
try:
    cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", validate="one_to_one")
except Exception:
    print("fail")

import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002", "C003"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="left", indicator=True)
anti = m.loc[m["_merge"] == "left_only", "cliente_id"].unique()
print(len(anti))

import pandas as pd
wide = pd.DataFrame({"id": ["a", "b"], "a": [1, 2], "b": [3, 4]})
long = wide.melt(id_vars="id", value_vars=["a", "b"])
print(len(long))

import pandas as pd
long = pd.DataFrame({"id": ["a", "a", "b", "b"], "k": ["m1", "m2", "m1", "m2"], "v": [1, 2, 3, 4]})
w = long.pivot_table(index="id", columns="k", values="v", aggfunc="sum").reset_index()
print(w.columns.tolist())

import pandas as pd
a = pd.DataFrame({"x": [1, 2]})
b = pd.DataFrame({"x": [3, 4]})
out = pd.concat([a, b], ignore_index=True)
print(len(out))

import pandas as pd
long = pd.DataFrame({"id": ["a", "a"], "periodo": ["m1", "m2"], "monto": [1, 2]})
w = long.pivot_table(index="id", columns="periodo", values="monto", aggfunc="sum")
w.columns = [f"{a}_{b}" if isinstance(w.columns, pd.MultiIndex) else str(c) for c in (w.columns if not isinstance(w.columns, pd.MultiIndex) else ["_".join(map(str, t)) for t in w.columns])]
# simple flatten for single-level after pivot
w.columns = [str(c) for c in w.columns]
print(list(w.columns))
exercises.note: Theory flatten MultiIndex; pivot here is single-level so str names.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": [1], "monto": [2.0]})
expected = {"cliente_id", "monto"}
print(set(df.columns) == expected)

import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1], "b": [2]})
out = df.rename(columns={"a": "monto"})
print(out.columns.tolist())

import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [10.0, 5.0, 3.0]})
print(df.groupby("region")["monto"].sum().to_dict())

import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [10.0, 5.0, 3.0]})
print(df.groupby("region")["monto"].transform("mean").tolist())

import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [10.0, 5.0, 3.0]})
out = df.groupby("region", as_index=False).agg(total=("monto", "sum"), n=("monto", "count"))
print(out.columns.tolist())

import pandas as pd
import math
s = pd.Series([1.0, 2.0, 3.0]).rolling(2).mean()
out = [None if (isinstance(v, float) and math.isnan(v)) else float(v) for v in s.tolist()]
print(out)
exercises.note: Theory rolling; first value nan as None.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "cliente_id": ["C001", "C001", "C002"],
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-15", "2024-03-01", "2024-02-01"]),
})
df["cohort"] = df.groupby("cliente_id")["fecha"].transform("min").dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
print(df.drop_duplicates("cliente_id").set_index("cliente_id")["cohort"].to_dict())
exercises.note: Hints groupby transform min + to_period M.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([1.0, 3.0, 5.0], index=pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "2024-01-02", "2024-01-03"]))
s = s.sort_index()
print(float(s.rolling(2).mean().iloc[-1]))
exercises.note: Theory sort before rolling; last mean window 2.

exercises.code:
parts = [10.0, 20.0, 70.0]
total = 100.0
print(abs(sum(parts) - total) < 1e-9)

pagados = 10
activos = 40
print(pagados / activos)

total = 100
lima = 60
print(total - lima)

import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-10", "2024-03-01"]),
    "monto": [10.0, 999.0],
})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-01-31")
print(tx.loc[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff, "monto"].tolist())

import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-10", "2024-03-01"]),
    "monto": [10.0, 999.0],
})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-01-31")
pre = float(tx.loc[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff, "monto"].sum())
total = float(tx["monto"].sum())
print(total - pre)

import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({
    "cliente_id": ["C001", "C001"],
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-10", "2024-03-01"]),
    "monto": [10.0, 999.0],
})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-01-31")
feat = tx[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff].groupby("cliente_id")["monto"].max()
print(feat.to_dict())
exercises.note: Hints filter then groupby max; demo uses sum analog.
